speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: Run to my lord of Suffolk. Let him know

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: We have dispatched the Duke as he commanded.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: O, that it were to do! What have we done?

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MURDERER
text: Didst ever hear a man so penitent?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: Here comes my lord.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Now, sirs, have you dispatched this thing?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: Ay, my good lord, he’s dead.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Why, that’s well said. Go, get you to my house;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: I will reward you for this venturous deed.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: The King and all the peers are here at hand.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Have you laid fair the bed? Is all things well,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: According as I gave directions?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              MURDERER
text: ’Tis, my good lord.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Away, be gone.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Go, call our uncle to our presence straight.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Say we intend to try his Grace today

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: If he be guilty, as ’tis publishèd.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: I’ll call him presently, my noble lord.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Lords, take your places; and, I pray you all,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Proceed no straiter ’gainst our uncle Gloucester

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Than from true evidence of good esteem

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: He be approved in practice culpable.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: God forbid any malice should prevail

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: That faultless may condemn a nobleman!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Pray God he may acquit him of suspicion!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I thank thee, Meg. These words content me much.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: How now? Why look’st thou pale? Why tremblest thou?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Where is our uncle? What’s the matter, Suffolk?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Dead in his bed, my lord. Gloucester is dead.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Marry, God forfend!

speaker: CARDINAL
text: God’s secret judgment. I did dream tonight

speaker: CARDINAL
text: The Duke was dumb and could not speak a word.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: How fares my lord? Help, lords, the King is dead!

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Rear up his body. Wring him by the nose.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Run, go, help, help! O Henry, ope thine eyes!

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: He doth revive again. Madam, be patient.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: O heavenly God!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: How fares my gracious lord?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Comfort, my sovereign! Gracious Henry, comfort!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: What, doth my lord of Suffolk comfort me?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Came he right now to sing a raven’s note,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Whose dismal tune bereft my vital powers,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And thinks he that the chirping of a wren,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: By crying comfort from a hollow breast,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Can chase away the first-conceivèd sound?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Hide not thy poison with such sugared words.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Lay not thy hands on me. Forbear, I say!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Their touch affrights me as a serpent’s sting.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Thou baleful messenger, out of my sight!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Upon thy eyeballs, murderous Tyranny

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Sits in grim majesty to fright the world.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Look not upon me, for thine eyes are wounding.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Yet do not go away. Come, basilisk,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And kill the innocent gazer with thy sight;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For in the shade of death I shall find joy,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: In life but double death, now Gloucester’s dead.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Why do you rate my lord of Suffolk thus?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Although the Duke was enemy to him,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Yet he most Christian-like laments his death.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And for myself, foe as he was to me,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Might liquid tears or heart-offending groans

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Or blood-consuming sighs recall his life,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: I would be blind with weeping, sick with groans,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Look pale as primrose with blood-drinking sighs,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And all to have the noble duke alive.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: What know I how the world may deem of me?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: For it is known we were but hollow friends.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: It may be judged I made the Duke away;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: So shall my name with slander’s tongue be wounded

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And princes’ courts be filled with my reproach.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: This get I by his death. Ay me, unhappy,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: To be a queen and crowned with infamy!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Ah, woe is me for Gloucester, wretched man!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Be woe for me, more wretched than he is.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: What, dost thou turn away and hide thy face?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: I am no loathsome leper. Look on me.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: What, art thou, like the adder, waxen deaf?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Be poisonous too, and kill thy forlorn queen.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Is all thy comfort shut in Gloucester’s tomb?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Why, then, Dame Margaret was ne’er thy joy.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Erect his statue and worship it,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And make my image but an alehouse sign.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Was I for this nigh-wracked upon the sea

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And twice by awkward wind from England’s bank

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Drove back again unto my native clime?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: What boded this, but well forewarning wind

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Did seem to say “Seek not a scorpion’s nest,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Nor set no footing on this unkind shore”?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: What did I then but cursed the gentle gusts

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And he that loosed them forth their brazen caves

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And bid them blow towards England’s blessèd shore

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Or turn our stern upon a dreadful rock?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Yet Aeolus would not be a murderer,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: But left that hateful office unto thee.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: The pretty-vaulting sea refused to drown me,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Knowing that thou wouldst have me drowned on shore

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: With tears as salt as sea, through thy unkindness.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: The splitting rocks cow’red in the sinking sands

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And would not dash me with their ragged sides

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Because thy flinty heart, more hard than they,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Might in thy palace perish Margaret.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: As far as I could ken thy chalky cliffs,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: When from thy shore the tempest beat us back,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: I stood upon the hatches in the storm,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And when the dusky sky began to rob

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: My earnest-gaping sight of thy land’s view,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: I took a costly jewel from my neck—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: A heart it was, bound in with diamonds—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And threw it towards thy land. The sea received it,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And so I wished thy body might my heart.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And even with this I lost fair England’s view,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And bid mine eyes be packing with my heart,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And called them blind and dusky spectacles

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: For losing ken of Albion’s wishèd coast.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: How often have I tempted Suffolk’s tongue,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: The agent of thy foul inconstancy,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: To sit and watch me, as Ascanius did

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: When he to madding Dido would unfold

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: His father’s acts commenced in burning Troy!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Am I not witched like her, or thou not false like him?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Ay me, I can no more. Die, Margaret,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: For Henry weeps that thou dost live so long.

speaker: WARWICK
text: It is reported, mighty sovereign,

speaker: WARWICK
text: That good Duke Humphrey traitorously is murdered

speaker: WARWICK
text: By Suffolk and the Cardinal Beaufort’s means.

speaker: WARWICK
text: The Commons, like an angry hive of bees

speaker: WARWICK
text: That want their leader, scatter up and down

speaker: WARWICK
text: And care not who they sting in his revenge.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Myself have calmed their spleenful mutiny,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Until they hear the order of his death.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That he is dead, good Warwick, ’tis too true;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But how he died God knows, not Henry.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Enter his chamber, view his breathless corpse,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And comment then upon his sudden death.

speaker: WARWICK
text: That shall I do, my liege.—Stay, Salisbury,

speaker: WARWICK
text: With the rude multitude till I return.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: O Thou that judgest all things, stay my thoughts,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: My thoughts that labor to persuade my soul

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Some violent hands were laid on Humphrey’s life.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: If my suspect be false, forgive me, God,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For judgment only doth belong to Thee.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Fain would I go to chafe his paly lips

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: With twenty thousand kisses, and to drain

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Upon his face an ocean of salt tears,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: To tell my love unto his dumb deaf trunk

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And with my fingers feel his hand unfeeling;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But all in vain are these mean obsequies.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And to survey his dead and earthy image,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: What were it but to make my sorrow greater?

speaker: WARWICK
text: Come hither, gracious sovereign. View this body.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That is to see how deep my grave is made,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For with his soul fled all my worldly solace;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For seeing him, I see my life in death.

speaker: WARWICK
text: As surely as my soul intends to live

speaker: WARWICK
text: With that dread King that took our state upon Him

speaker: WARWICK
text: To free us from His Father’s wrathful curse,

speaker: WARWICK
text: I do believe that violent hands were laid

speaker: WARWICK
text: Upon the life of this thrice-famèd duke.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: A dreadful oath, sworn with a solemn tongue!

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: What instance gives Lord Warwick for his vow?

speaker: WARWICK
text: See how the blood is settled in his face.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Oft have I seen a timely-parted ghost,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Of ashy semblance, meager, pale, and bloodless,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Being all descended to the laboring heart,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Who, in the conflict that it holds with death,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Attracts the same for aidance ’gainst the enemy,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Which with the heart there cools and ne’er returneth

speaker: WARWICK
text: To blush and beautify the cheek again.

speaker: WARWICK
text: But see, his face is black and full of blood;

speaker: WARWICK
text: His eyeballs further out than when he lived,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Staring full ghastly, like a strangled man;

speaker: WARWICK
text: His hair upreared, his nostrils stretched with struggling;

speaker: WARWICK
text: His hands abroad displayed, as one that grasped

speaker: WARWICK
text: And tugged for life and was by strength subdued.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Look, on the sheets his hair, you see, is sticking;

speaker: WARWICK
text: His well-proportioned beard made rough and rugged,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Like to the summer’s corn by tempest lodged.

speaker: WARWICK
text: It cannot be but he was murdered here.

speaker: WARWICK
text: The least of all these signs were probable.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Why, Warwick, who should do the Duke to death?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Myself and Beaufort had him in protection,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And we, I hope, sir, are no murderers.

speaker: WARWICK
text: But both of you were vowed Duke Humphrey’s foes,

speaker: WARWICK
text:
To
                 
                Cardinal
                .
                 
              And you, forsooth, had the good duke to keep.

speaker: WARWICK
text: ’Tis like you would not feast him like a friend,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And ’tis well seen he found an enemy.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Then you, belike, suspect these noblemen

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: As guilty of Duke Humphrey’s timeless death.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Who finds the heifer dead and bleeding fresh,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And sees fast by a butcher with an ax,

speaker: WARWICK
text: But will suspect ’twas he that made the slaughter?

speaker: WARWICK
text: Who finds the partridge in the puttock’s nest

speaker: WARWICK
text: But may imagine how the bird was dead,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Although the kite soar with unbloodied beak?

speaker: WARWICK
text: Even so suspicious is this tragedy.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Are you the butcher, Suffolk? Where’s your knife?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Is Beaufort termed a kite? Where are his talons?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: I wear no knife to slaughter sleeping men,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: But here’s a vengeful sword, rusted with ease,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: That shall be scoured in his rancorous heart

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: That slanders me with murder’s crimson badge.—

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Say, if thou dar’st, proud lord of Warwickshire,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: That I am faulty in Duke Humphrey’s death.

speaker: WARWICK
text: What dares not Warwick, if false Suffolk dare him?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: He dares not calm his contumelious spirit

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Nor cease to be an arrogant controller,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Though Suffolk dare him twenty thousand times.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Madam, be still—with reverence may I say—

speaker: WARWICK
text: For every word you speak in his behalf

speaker: WARWICK
text: Is slander to your royal dignity.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Blunt-witted lord, ignoble in demeanor!

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: If ever lady wronged her lord so much,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Thy mother took into her blameful bed

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Some stern untutored churl, and noble stock

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Was graft with crab-tree slip, whose fruit thou art

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And never of the Nevilles’ noble race.

speaker: WARWICK
text: But that the guilt of murder bucklers thee

speaker: WARWICK
text: And I should rob the deathsman of his fee,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Quitting thee thereby of ten thousand shames,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And that my sovereign’s presence makes me mild,

speaker: WARWICK
text: I would, false murd’rous coward, on thy knee

speaker: WARWICK
text: Make thee beg pardon for thy passèd speech

speaker: WARWICK
text: And say it was thy mother that thou meant’st,

speaker: WARWICK
text: That thou thyself wast born in bastardy;

speaker: WARWICK
text: And after all this fearful homage done,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Give thee thy hire and send thy soul to hell,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Pernicious bloodsucker of sleeping men!

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Thou shalt be waking while I shed thy blood,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: If from this presence thou dar’st go with me.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Away even now, or I will drag thee hence!

speaker: WARWICK
text: Unworthy though thou art, I’ll cope with thee

speaker: WARWICK
text: And do some service to Duke Humphrey’s ghost.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: What stronger breastplate than a heart untainted?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Thrice is he armed that hath his quarrel just,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And he but naked, though locked up in steel,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Whose conscience with injustice is corrupted.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: What noise is this?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Why, how now, lords? Your wrathful weapons drawn

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Here in our presence? Dare you be so bold?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Why, what tumultuous clamor have we here?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: The trait’rous Warwick, with the men of Bury,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Set all upon me, mighty sovereign.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Sirs, stand apart. The King shall know your mind.—

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Dread lord, the Commons send you word by me,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Unless Lord Suffolk straight be done to death

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Or banishèd fair England’s territories,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: They will by violence tear him from your palace

speaker: SALISBURY
text: And torture him with grievous ling’ring death.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: They say, by him the good duke Humphrey died;

speaker: SALISBURY
text: They say, in him they fear your Highness’ death;

speaker: SALISBURY
text: And mere instinct of love and loyalty,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Free from a stubborn opposite intent,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: As being thought to contradict your liking,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Makes them thus forward in his banishment.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: They say, in care of your most royal person,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: That if your Highness should intend to sleep,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: And charge that no man should disturb your rest,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: In pain of your dislike or pain of death,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Yet, notwithstanding such a strait edict,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Were there a serpent seen with forkèd tongue

speaker: SALISBURY
text: That slyly glided towards your Majesty,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: It were but necessary you were waked,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Lest, being suffered in that harmful slumber,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: The mortal worm might make the sleep eternal.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: And therefore do they cry, though you forbid,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: That they will guard you, whe’er you will or no,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: From such fell serpents as false Suffolk is,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: With whose envenomèd and fatal sting

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Your loving uncle, twenty times his worth,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: They say, is shamefully bereft of life.

speaker: COMMONS
text: An answer from the King, my lord of Salisbury!

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: ’Tis like the Commons, rude unpolished hinds,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Could send such message to their sovereign!

speaker: SUFFOLK
text:
To
                 
                Salisbury
                .
                 
              But you, my lord, were glad to be employed,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: To show how quaint an orator you are.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: But all the honor Salisbury hath won

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Is that he was the lord ambassador

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Sent from a sort of tinkers to the King.

speaker: COMMONS
text: An answer from the King, or we will all break in.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Go, Salisbury, and tell them all from me,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I thank them for their tender loving care;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And, had I not been cited so by them,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Yet did I purpose as they do entreat.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: For, sure, my thoughts do hourly prophesy

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Mischance unto my state by Suffolk’s means.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And therefore, by His Majesty I swear,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Whose far unworthy deputy I am,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: He shall not breathe infection in this air

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But three days longer, on the pain of death.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: O Henry, let me plead for gentle Suffolk!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Ungentle queen to call him gentle Suffolk!

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: No more, I say. If thou dost plead for him,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Thou wilt but add increase unto my wrath.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Had I but said, I would have kept my word;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: But when I swear, it is irrevocable.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text:
To
                 
                Suffolk
                .
                 
              If, after three days’ space, thou here be’st found

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: On any ground that I am ruler of,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The world shall not be ransom for thy life.—

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Come, Warwick, come, good Warwick, go with me.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I have great matters to impart to thee.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Mischance and sorrow go along with you!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Heart’s discontent and sour affliction

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Be playfellows to keep you company!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: There’s two of you; the devil make a third,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And threefold vengeance tend upon your steps!

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Cease, gentle queen, these execrations,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And let thy Suffolk take his heavy leave.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Fie, coward woman and soft-hearted wretch!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Hast thou not spirit to curse thine enemies?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: A plague upon them! Wherefore should I curse them?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Could curses kill, as doth the mandrake’s groan,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: I would invent as bitter searching terms,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: As curst, as harsh, and horrible to hear,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Delivered strongly through my fixèd teeth,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: With full as many signs of deadly hate,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: As lean-faced Envy in her loathsome cave.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: My tongue should stumble in mine earnest words;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Mine eyes should sparkle like the beaten flint;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Mine hair be fixed on end, as one distract;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Ay, every joint should seem to curse and ban;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And even now my burdened heart would break

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Should I not curse them. Poison be their drink!

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Gall, worse than gall, the daintiest that they taste;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Their sweetest shade, a grove of cypress trees;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Their chiefest prospect, murd’ring basilisks;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Their softest touch, as smart as lizards’ stings!

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Their music, frightful as the serpent’s hiss,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And boding screech owls make the consort full!

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: All the foul terrors in dark-seated hell—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Enough, sweet Suffolk, thou torment’st thyself,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And these dread curses, like the sun ’gainst glass,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Or like an over-chargèd gun, recoil

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And turn the force of them upon thyself.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: You bade me ban, and will you bid me leave?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Now, by the ground that I am banished from,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Well could I curse away a winter’s night,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Though standing naked on a mountain top

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Where biting cold would never let grass grow,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And think it but a minute spent in sport.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: O, let me entreat thee cease! Give me thy hand,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: That I may dew it with my mournful tears;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Nor let the rain of heaven wet this place

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: To wash away my woeful monuments.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: O, could this kiss be printed in thy hand,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: That thou mightst think upon these by the seal,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Through whom a thousand sighs are breathed for thee!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: So, get thee gone, that I may know my grief;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: ’Tis but surmised whiles thou art standing by,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: As one that surfeits thinking on a want.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: I will repeal thee, or, be well assured,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Adventure to be banishèd myself;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And banishèd I am, if but from thee.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Go, speak not to me. Even now be gone!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: O, go not yet! Even thus two friends condemned

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Embrace and kiss and take ten thousand leaves,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Loather a hundred times to part than die.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Yet now farewell, and farewell life with thee.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Thus is poor Suffolk ten times banishèd,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Once by the King, and three times thrice by thee.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: ’Tis not the land I care for, wert thou thence.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: A wilderness is populous enough,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: So Suffolk had thy heavenly company;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: For where thou art, there is the world itself,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: With every several pleasure in the world;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And where thou art not, desolation.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: I can no more. Live thou to joy thy life;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Myself no joy in naught but that thou liv’st.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Whither goes Vaux so fast? What news, I prithee?

speaker: VAUX
text: To signify unto his Majesty,

speaker: VAUX
text: That Cardinal Beaufort is at point of death;

speaker: VAUX
text: For suddenly a grievous sickness took him

speaker: VAUX
text: That makes him gasp and stare and catch the air,

speaker: VAUX
text: Blaspheming God and cursing men on Earth.

speaker: VAUX
text: Sometimes he talks as if Duke Humphrey’s ghost

speaker: VAUX
text: Were by his side; sometimes he calls the King

speaker: VAUX
text: And whispers to his pillow, as to him,

speaker: VAUX
text: The secrets of his overchargèd soul.

speaker: VAUX
text: And I am sent to tell his Majesty

speaker: VAUX
text: That even now he cries aloud for him.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Go, tell this heavy message to the King.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Ay me! What is this world? What news are these!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: But wherefore grieve I at an hour’s poor loss,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Omitting Suffolk’s exile, my soul’s treasure?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Why only, Suffolk, mourn I not for thee,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And with the southern clouds contend in tears—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Theirs for the earth’s increase, mine for my sorrows’?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Now get thee hence. The King, thou know’st, is coming;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: If thou be found by me, thou art but dead.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: If I depart from thee, I cannot live;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And in thy sight to die, what were it else

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: But like a pleasant slumber in thy lap?

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Here could I breathe my soul into the air,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: As mild and gentle as the cradle babe

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Dying with mother’s dug between its lips;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Where, from thy sight, I should be raging mad

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And cry out for thee to close up mine eyes,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: To have thee with thy lips to stop my mouth.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: So shouldst thou either turn my flying soul,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Or I should breathe it so into thy body,

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: And then it lived in sweet Elysium.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: To die by thee were but to die in jest;

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: From thee to die were torture more than death.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: O, let me stay, befall what may befall!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Away! Though parting be a fretful corrosive,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: It is applièd to a deathful wound.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: To France, sweet Suffolk. Let me hear from thee,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: For wheresoe’er thou art in this world’s globe,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: I’ll have an Iris that shall find thee out.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: I go.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And take my heart with thee.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: A jewel locked into the woefull’st cask

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: That ever did contain a thing of worth!

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: Even as a splitted bark, so sunder we.

speaker: SUFFOLK
text: This way fall I to death.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: This way for me.
